evidence: The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.
Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.
Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.
In a world of AI-generated everything, the only reliable signal is direct interaction over time.
Ivor's new Head of Strategic Ops will reach out after joining (week after next). Let me see who else I know at Handshake.

source_display: The 'Quiet Part' Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: Employers have selection criteria they cannot legally state. The gap between stated job requirements and actual selection preferences is a structural feature of hiring markets, not a bug.

source_display: Information Efficiency Has Inverted Recruiter Value
evidence: I have nothing to give these people besides the resume. All of these people look the same.

source_display: Response Rate as the Only Metric That Matters
evidence: If Careerspan's candidates respond at higher rates than other sources, that's sufficient proof of value. Everything else just ladders up to this single metric.

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Proposition
evidence: What we are is talent scanning, and that's literally the only thing that matters.

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Proposition
evidence: This reframes product priorities, marketing messaging, and technical roadmap. Decided by V with Ilse's acknowledgment that this is now the go-forward direction.

In talent marketplaces, having the name of a prestigious firm on your website may be worth more than their actual business contribution. Superposition wouldn't bring users. They might not even bring significant revenue. But their logo creates a trust cascade.
V's counter to 'you only have 500 users' was essentially: 'Yes, but they're the right 500.' This inverts the network effects playbook.
Logan's biotech friend disrupted her industry by going directly to morgues instead of through middlemen. 'My middleman is charging me too much money' → go direct to source.

Pursue both brand-name partnerships (Superposition) for credibility AND hungry individual recruiters for execution. Brand names provide website legitimacy; individual recruiters drive actual success stories.
